name: Verapamil

dosing: Most patients end up at 240-480mg/day, though some require doses as high as 960mg/day to achieve suppression
Standard immediate-release tablets are preferred over extended-release formulations
Many in the community find verapamil insufficient as a sole preventive and combine it with other treatments
Requires EKG/ECG monitoring before each dose increase and periodically at high doses due to cardiac risks
preparations: Taken as oral immediate-release tablets divided into 2-3 doses per day
Standard release is strongly preferred; extended-release versions are less effective for cluster headache
schedule: Start low at 80-120mg/day and titrate up by 80mg every 10-14 days until attacks are suppressed or maximum tolerated dose is reached
Often paired with a prednisone taper at cycle onset to bridge the 2-4 weeks verapamil needs to take full effect
Continue for the full cycle plus 4-6 weeks after attacks stop, then taper off gradually; chronic CH patients may stay on it long-term

sideEffects: Risk of heart damage requiring pacemaker at high doses long-term
